whatItDoes: Generates HTTP Basic Authentication headers from a username and password. Enter your credentials to instantly get the complete Authorization header value in the standard 'Authorization: Basic base64encoded' format, ready to use in API requests.
whyItExists: HTTP Basic Auth requires base64-encoding the username:password string. Manually computing this with command-line tools or online converters adds friction when testing APIs that use Basic authentication.
whoShouldUse: API developers testing endpoints with Basic Auth, QA engineers writing API tests, and backend developers debugging authentication issues.
useCases: Generate a Basic Auth header to test a protected API endpoint with curl or Postman
Quickly create an Authorization header value for HTTP debugging tools
Verify that a base64-encoded credential matches what a server expects
Generate test credentials for integration testing
instructions: Enter the username in the Username field
Enter the password in the Password field
The Authorization header is generated instantly
Copy the full header or just the base64 value
bestPractices: Use Copy Header for the full 'Authorization: Basic ...' string
Use Copy Value Only when you just need the base64-encoded part
Never hardcode credentials in source code — use environment variables
Consider token-based auth (OAuth, JWT) over Basic Auth for production
commonMistakes: Forgetting the space between 'Basic' and the base64 value in the header
Using URL-encoded passwords — Basic Auth expects the raw password
Confusing Basic Auth with Bearer token authentication
faq: Is Basic Auth secure? — Only over HTTPS. The base64 encoding is not encryption — it's trivially reversible.
Does it handle special characters in passwords? — Yes, all characters are correctly base64-encoded.
Can I use this for OAuth or Bearer tokens? — No, this tool specifically generates Basic Auth headers.
